metadata.title: The Box Under the Floor
metadata.setting: A quiet small town in the present day, centred on an old abandoned house that holds a fifty-year-old secret.

characters.name: Kai
characters.description: A 19-year-old with short brown hair and an honest face who takes odd jobs to earn money but cannot stop himself from investigating things he should leave alone.
characters.flaw: too curious — he cannot ignore a mystery, even when stopping would be the safer choice
characters.current_state: standing still on the road away from the Old House, stunned after reading 'HOLT' scratched inside the metal lid
key_items: old video camera (taken from the back-room cupboard, placed in his bag)
house key given by Mr. Holt
metal box (retrieved from beneath the cracked floorboard)
small key on a short metal chain (found inside the metal box)
old photograph — two adults and a young child standing in front of the Old House (found inside the metal box)

characters.name: Mr. Holt
characters.role: secondary — employer, significance to grow in later episodes
characters.description: A quiet man in his sixties with grey hair and careful, watchful eyes who hired Kai to clean out the old house.
characters.flaw: secretive — he shares as little information as possible
characters.current_state: not present; name scratched inside the hidden metal box — connection to the house now confirmed

characters.name: Mrs. Webb
characters.role: secondary — neighbour, significance to grow in Episode 4
characters.description: A woman with a wide, flat face and small eyes that miss nothing; lives near the Old House and has long knowledge of its history.
characters.flaw: guarded — shares warnings but withholds the reasons behind them
characters.current_state: suspicious but cooperative; knows more about the Old House than she chose to say

locations.name: The Old House
locations.description: A two-storey wooden house on the edge of town, empty for many years, with dusty rooms, creaking floors, and boards nailed over some of the windows.

locations.name: The Back Room
locations.description: A small bare room at the back of the old house with old wooden floorboards, a single built-in cupboard against the wall, and one dirty window facing the yard. The cracked floorboard in the corner nearest the window has now been pried up and the gap beneath it is empty.

locations.name: The Town
locations.description: A quiet small town where everyone knows each other and old stories are still passed between neighbours.

locations.name: Mrs. Webb's House
locations.description: A house near the Old House with a front yard full of old tools and broken things; home of Mrs. Webb, a neighbour who has long knowledge of the Old House's troubled history.

episode_history.summary: Kai begins cleaning the back room and finds an old video camera hidden in the built-in cupboard. The camera's tape shows a frightened family hiding a metal box beneath a floorboard in that same room — Kai searches every board but cannot identify the correct one. As he leaves, he notices a long straight drag mark in the hallway dust running from the foot of the staircase to the front door — a mark that was not there when he arrived.

Kai replays the tape slowly and identifies the cracked floorboard in the corner of the back room nearest the window as the correct hiding spot by matching a detail on the tape to the real floor. He also finds a wide, flat bootprint near the cupboard that is not his, confirming someone else entered the room after his last visit; a phone call to Mr. Holt yields only a curt dismissal. When Kai tries to lift the board with his screwdriver, it will not move — the board is nailed too tight — and he leaves the house knowing he must return with a crowbar.

Kai borrows a crowbar from his neighbour Mrs. Webb, who hands it over with the warning 'That old house brings trouble — it always has.' Back in the Old House, a loud creak from directly above him while he forces the floorboard makes him stop and wait in silence; when no further sound comes, he works faster and pries the board free, retrieving the metal box from the gap below. Outside on the road, he opens the lid and finds a small key on a chain and an old photograph of two adults and a young child standing in front of the Old House; on the inside of the lid, the name HOLT is scratched deep into the metal — his employer's name.

unresolved_threads.description: Who is the frightened family on the tape, and when did they hide the metal box? The old photograph of two adults and a child outside the Old House tightens this question — and the name HOLT scratched on the lid connects the box directly to Kai's employer.
Who made the long drag mark in the hallway dust and the wide bootprint in the back room — and was this same person responsible for the creak heard from upstairs during Kai's visit in Episode 3?
What does the small key found inside the metal box open?

series_plan.central_question: What is hidden inside the metal box under the floorboards, and who does it truly belong to?

arc_beats.beat: Kai finds the video camera and watches the tape; he sees a frightened family hiding a metal box under a floorboard in the very room he is standing in, and he begins searching for the right board.
Kai locates the correct floorboard but cannot lift it alone; he also finds fresh evidence that someone else has been inside the house very recently, raising the danger of his search.
Kai opens the box and finds a small key and an old photograph; the name 'HOLT' scratched on the lid connects the hidden secret directly to his employer.

An old neighbour recognises the photograph and tells Kai that the Holt family once owned the house and that the father disappeared with money that was not his; Kai begins to suspect Mr. Holt is the son trying to recover the box.
Mr. Holt discovers Kai has the box and demands it back; Kai uses the key to reach the hidden contents at the old post office before Mr. Holt can stop him.

The contents of the post office box prove that Mr. Holt's father stole the house from another family fifty years ago; the rightful owner is found, the documents are returned, and all secrets are answered.

from_targets: force — to push something with great strength so that it moves (used as 'tried to force the board up'; 'He pushed down hard and tried to force the board up'; 'forced it down again')

rusty — covered in red-brown marks caused by old water and air (used as 'It was old and rusty, orange-brown along its length'; 'slid the rusty crowbar under its edge'; reinforced by 'A rusted bucket sat near the gate' in the same scene)
lid — the removable top cover of a box (used as 'He held the box in both hands and opened the lid'; 'He turned the lid over and looked at the inside surface')
scratch — to cut letters or marks into a surface with something sharp (used as 'Letters were scratched into the metal, cut deep with something sharp')
photograph — a flat picture made by a camera, usually printed on paper (used as 'And an old photograph — two adults and a young child standing in front of the Old House')

vocabulary_taught: floorboard — a single long piece of wood that makes up a floor
tape — a recorded video on a small cassette inside a camera
frightened — very scared, showing fear on the face and body
screen — the small flat surface on the camera that shows the video
nail — to hammer a nail through wood to fix it in place (verb use)
drag — to pull something heavy slowly across the floor
crack — a thin break or line running across a hard surface
footprint — the mark a shoe or boot leaves in dust or soft ground
corner — the place inside a room where two walls meet
tight — fixed very firmly so it cannot easily move
crowbar — a heavy metal bar used to force open or lift something
force — to push something with great strength so that it moves
rusty — covered in red-brown marks caused by old water and air
lid — the removable top cover of a box
scratch — to cut letters or marks into a surface with something sharp
photograph — a flat picture made by a camera, usually printed on paper
last_scene_position: Kai is standing on the road in the town (loc_003), a short distance from the Old House, holding the open metal box in both hands. He has just read the letters H-O-L-T scratched into the inside of the lid and is holding the small key on its chain between his fingers; the old photograph of two adults and a young child standing in front of the Old House is also inside the box. He has not moved. Unresolved in the scene: the identity of the people in the photograph, what the small key opens, and the nature of Mr. Holt's connection to the hidden box. The next episode must begin after this moment of stillness — Kai must act on what he has found.
